tool_args.body: Offering: Premium Support (24x7, T25) Summary: Bridge escalation workflow for Premium Support SLA Tier 25. Resolution Steps: 1) Verify paging and alerting systems are operational. 2) Start bridge within ≤10 min of escalation trigger. 3) Assign roles: Bridge Manager, Technical Lead, Communications Lead. 4) Engage vendors as needed per comms matrix. 5) Complete 72h RCA post-resolution. Rules: Follow comms matrix strictly. Workarounds: Manual escalation path if automated paging fails — contact on-call manager directly. Policies/Process: KPI drills quarterly. Escalation Path: L1 → L2 → Bridge Manager → Tier 25 Premium Support Lead. Case Reference: CS-0000048 (Zebra Partners, high priority, response SLA breached — bridge escalation initiated).

tool_args.title: Recovery article for Palo Alto Networks PA-3220
tool_args.body:
## Recovery Procedures for Palo Alto Networks PA-3220

### Overview
This article documents the recovery procedures for the Palo Alto Networks PA-3220 next-generation firewall appliance. Follow these steps to recover a PA-3220 device that has become unresponsive, failed to boot, or requires a factory reset.

### Prerequisites
- Console cable (RJ-45 to DB-9 or USB-to-Serial adapter)
- Terminal emulation software (PuTTY, Tera Term, etc.) with settings: 9600 baud, 8 data bits, 1 stop bit, no parity, no flow control
- PAN-OS software image file (matching the target version)
- USB flash drive (FAT32 formatted, minimum 4GB) for image transfer if needed
- Physical access to the PA-3220 appliance

### Recovery Procedure 1: Boot into Maintenance Mode
1. Connect the console cable to the Console port on the PA-3220 front panel.
2. Power on or reboot the device.
3. During boot, watch the console output and press 'm' when prompted to enter Maintenance Mode.
4. In Maintenance Mode, you can:
   - Reinstall PAN-OS from an image
   - Perform a factory reset
   - Access diagnostic tools

### Recovery Procedure 2: Factory Reset via Maintenance Mode
1. Boot into Maintenance Mode as described above.
2. Select the 'Factory Reset' option from the maintenance menu.
3. Confirm the reset when prompted.
4. The device will wipe its configuration and reboot with factory default settings.
5. Default credentials: admin / admin
6. Default IP: 192.168.1.1

### Recovery Procedure 3: PAN-OS Reinstallation
1. Prepare a USB drive with the PAN-OS image file.
2. Insert the USB drive into the PA-3220 USB port.
3. Boot into Maintenance Mode.
4. Select 'Install from USB' and choose the PAN-OS image file.
5. Follow the on-screen prompts to complete the installation.
6. After installation, the device will reboot automatically.

### Recovery Procedure 4: Password Recovery
1. Connect via console cable and reboot the device.
2. During boot, press 'm' to enter Maintenance Mode.
3. Select the password recovery option.
4. Follow the prompts to reset the admin password.
5. Reboot the device and log in with the new password.

### Post-Recovery Steps
1. Verify the device boots successfully and all interfaces are recognized.
2. Restore configuration from backup if available.
3. Verify security policies and NAT rules are functioning.
4. Check that threat prevention and URL filtering subscriptions are active.
5. Test connectivity through the firewall.

### Troubleshooting
- If the device fails to enter Maintenance Mode, verify console cable connection and terminal settings.
- If USB installation fails, try a different USB drive or re-download the PAN-OS image.
- For persistent boot failures, contact Palo Alto Networks TAC for hardware diagnostics.

### Related Documentation
- Palo Alto Networks PA-3220 Hardware Reference Guide
- PAN-OS Administrator's Guide
- TechCorp Internal Network Recovery Playbook

tool_args.title: Recovery article for Palo Alto Networks PA-3220
tool_args.body: Recovery procedures for PA-3220: Boot into Maintenance Mode via console (9600-8-N-1), factory reset, PAN-OS reinstall via USB, or password recovery.
